meta.name: Ezoic Brand Standards — Vol. 02 / 2026, light edition
meta.note: Distilled for the canonical Next.js + Tailwind app. If this file and brand-site/index.html ever disagree, index.html (and the live catalog at iheartezoic.com) wins — re-run the distillation.

green.usage: Fills, the mark, primary actions only. Measures 2.8:1 on Paper — never used as small text on light surfaces.

ink.usage: Primary text; near-black dark-band surfaces (footer, full-bleed bands, .dlcta).

graphite.usage: Secondary text, metadata, and the accessible stand-in for 'accent text' on light surfaces (the source's --green-text actually resolves to this, not literal green).

steel.usage: Muted borders/captions; secondary text on dark surfaces.

paper.usage: Page background, light panels, and text-on-dark. There is no pure #FFFFFF anywhere in this system.

fontDisplay.usage: Headlines, h1–h6, large type specimens. Self-hosted, optical size 28pt. Static weights loaded: 600 / 700 / 900.

fontText.usage: Body copy and UI. Self-hosted, optical size 18pt. Static weights loaded: 300 / 400 / 500 / 600 / 700.

fontMono.usage: Code, file paths, hex values, exact values ONLY. Never decorative, never a heading or label face. Variable weight 200–900.

trackingBody.usage: Everything else — body, UI, labels, nav, buttons.

default.usage: Cards, panels, inputs, tables — square, hairline-edged editorial system.

full.usage: Pill buttons only — the one rounded shape in the entire system.

float.usage: Floating/overlay surfaces only (toast, mobile sheet panel) — never on default flat surfaces.

gapsRequiringBrandSignoff: No approved destructive/error color exists in the 5-hex palette. A non-brand utility red (#9A3324) is provided as a placeholder — confirm before shipping any destructive UI.
No approved dark mode/theme exists. The system is explicitly 'light, always' as of Vol. 02 / 2026 — the ink dark-bands are local accents, not a global theme.
No approved data-visualization/chart palette exists. The chart tokens here are a conservative derivation (one green highlight + neutrals), not an approved standard.
